CREATE TABLE test_lists (id INTEGER, numbers LIST<INTEGER>);
SELECT id, array_pop_front(numbers) AS updated_numbers FROM test_lists;
SELECT id, array_pop_front(numbers) AS updated_numbers FROM test_lists WHERE id = 2;
SELECT id, array_pop_front(numbers) AS updated_numbers FROM test_lists ORDER BY id DESC;
SELECT A.id, array_pop_front(B.numbers) AS updated_numbers FROM test_lists A JOIN test_lists B ON A.id = B.id WHERE A.id > 1;
CREATE TABLE additional_lists (group_id INTEGER, values LIST<INTEGER>);
SELECT A.group_id, array_pop_front(A.values) AS updated_values, array_pop_front(B.numbers) AS other_values FROM additional_lists A JOIN test_lists B ON A.group_id = 1;
SELECT id, array_pop_front(array_pop_front(numbers)) AS double_updated FROM test_lists;
WITH temp AS (SELECT id, array_pop_front(numbers) AS single_updated FROM test_lists) SELECT id, array_pop_front(single_updated) AS double_updated FROM temp;
SELECT id, CASE WHEN array_length(numbers) > 1 THEN array_pop_front(numbers) ELSE NULL END AS updated_numbers FROM test_lists;
SELECT id, array_pop_front(numbers) AS updated_numbers FROM test_lists WHERE array_length(numbers) > 0;
SELECT id, array_pop_front(numbers) AS updated_numbers FROM test_lists WHERE array_length(numbers) = 0;
SELECT id, array_pop_front(numbers) AS updated_numbers FROM test_lists LIMIT 2;
